title: NOC 72205 – Telecommunications equipment installation and cable television service technicians | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 72205 professionals are the essential "Last Mile" technicians who bring digital connectivity into Canadian homes and businesses. As a TEER 2 occupation, these technicians install, test, and repair a wide range of telecommunications equipment, including residential Wi-Fi systems, business VoIP networks, and cable television services. In 2026, as Canada rolls out Wi-Fi 7 and integrates Smart Home Hubs as a standard utility, these technicians are classified as High-Priority Service Talent. For immigration, this role is a strong candidate for the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and the OINP Employer Job Offer stream.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level2Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group722 – Electrical trades and electrical power line and cable workersMinor Group7220 – Electricians (except industrial and power system)NOC Code72205

duties: Installation: Install, arrange, remove, and maintain various types of telecommunications equipment and systems in residential and commercial settings.
Cabling: Pull and terminate internal wiring (Ethernet, Coaxial, Fiber) through walls and ceilings; install outlets and connection points.
Configuration: Configure and test equipment settings for high-speed internet, digital television, and phone services.
Troubleshooting: Use signal meters, voltmeters, and specialized diagnostic software to locate and repair faults in equipment or internal wiring.
Customer Education: Explain the operation of newly installed systems and devices to customers and provide basic technical support.
Reporting: Document installation details, signal strength readings, and materials used in company work orders and inventory systems.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is mandatory.

Education & Training:Completion of a three-to-four-year apprenticeship program OR a two-year college program in electronics or telecommunications.Alternatively, a combination of over three years of work experience and industry-related specialized courses may be accepted.
Completion of a three-to-four-year apprenticeship program OR a two-year college program in electronics or telecommunications.
Alternatively, a combination of over three years of work experience and industry-related specialized courses may be accepted.
Certifications:A valid driver’s license is almost always a mandatory requirement for field roles.Many employers require background checks and bondability for home entry.
A valid driver’s license is almost always a mandatory requirement for field roles.
Many employers require background checks and bondability for home entry.
Physical ability to use ladders and work in tight spaces (crawls/attics), along with good manual dexterity, is essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, experienced Service Technicians and Team Leads in urban centers often earn annual salaries between $75,000 and $90,000 inclusive of performance bonuses and overtime.
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable. While some basic installations are becoming "Self-Serve," the increasing complexity of home automation, security integration, and multi-gigabit fiber networks ensures a constant need for professional field technicians. Projections indicate a steady demand for technicians capable of maintaining the aging copper-to-fiber transition infrastructure through the end of the decade.
labour_market_demand: The demand for telecommunications equipment technicians in 2026 is Good to Very Good. In Ontario (GTA), British Columbia (Vancouver), and Alberta (Calgary/Edmonton), a surge in residential housing completions has created a high volume of "First-Time Installation" contracts. According to 2026 Job Bank data, while the market is balanced, there is a specific shortage of technicians who are cross-trained in 5G fixed wireless access (FWA) and integrated security system installations.
The Smart Home Frontier: Lead the installation of the next generation of IoT (Internet of Things) devices and high-capacity home networks.
Excellent Full-Time Prospects: Over 91.2% of workers in this NOC are employed in permanent, full-time positions with major national providers (Rogers, Bell, TELUS, Shaw).
Dynamic Work Environment: Enjoy a role that balances technical troubleshooting with high levels of customer interaction and field mobility.
Strategic PR Pathways: This NOC is frequently included in provincial "In-Demand" lists, making it a reliable route for a provincial nomination which adds 600 points to your Express Entry profile.
pathways: Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): The primary route for technicians with at least 2 years of work experience and a valid job offer or certificate of qualification.
OINP Employer Job Offer (Foreign Worker Stream): Ideal for those with a job offer from a major Canadian telecommunications firm or authorized contractor.
BC PNP Skilled Worker: Priority for technicians supporting the province’s digital infrastructure in high-growth urban corridors.
New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A temporary measure for 33,000 skilled workers already in Canada, targeting "Essential Service and Infrastructure" roles.
